seed_instruction: Write a step-by-step plan for hosting a virtual conference, with pre-event, live, and post-event tasks.
org_constraint: The response must include bullet points.
new_constraint: The response must not include bullet points.
instruction: Write a step-by-step plan for hosting a virtual conference, with pre-event, live, and post-event tasks. The response must include bullet points. The response must not include bullet points.
response:
Pre-Event Planning Phase

Secure your conference platform and hosting capabilities, ensuring it can handle expected attendee numbers and features like breakout rooms and live chat. Define your theme, goals, and target audience early on to tailor content and marketing. Develop a detailed agenda, assigning speakers and securing their presentations well in advance. Create a comprehensive marketing and registration strategy, utilizing email campaigns, social media promotion, and a dedicated conference website. Set up your registration system and establish a clear payment and cancellation policy. Identify and recruit session moderators and technical support staff. Create a detailed run-of-show document outlining every technical cue and speaker transition for the day of the event. Conduct technical dry runs with all key speakers and presenters to test audio, video, and slide sharing capabilities.

Live Event Execution Phase

On the day of the conference, ensure all technical staff are logged in and ready to troubleshoot immediately. Begin with a clear opening session that sets the tone, reviews the agenda, and provides a tech walkthrough for attendees. Manage the schedule strictly, using clear timekeeping alerts to keep sessions moving. During sessions, monitor the live chat and Q&A channels, flagging important questions for speakers or moderators to address. Have designated moderators actively manage breakout rooms, ensuring discussions stay on topic and managing the flow of interaction. Be prepared to handle technical issues seamlessly, having backup plans or readily available tech support to resolve interruptions quickly. Maintain consistent communication with attendees throughout the event via announcements or dedicated help channels.

Post-Event Follow-up Phase

Immediately following the conference, send a thank-you email to all attendees, speakers, and sponsors, providing access to recordings of the sessions and any promised materials. Distribute a concise feedback survey to gather insights on content, platform usability, and overall experience. Send personalized follow-up communications to sponsors, providing them with metrics and access to any lead generation data as promised. Analyze the collected data, including registration numbers, attendance rates per session, and survey responses, to create a detailed post-mortem report. Compile and share a summary of the conference highlights and key takeaways across your marketing channels to maintain momentum and build anticipation for the next event. Finally, debrief with your core team to document lessons learned regarding what worked well and what needs improvement for future virtual conferences.<turn|>
